title: 1. The necessity of asynchronous execution in multi-agent routing

blocks.content: Executing multi-agent tasks synchronously blocks connection threads. If Agent A calls Agent B, which calls Agent C, any network jitter or model delay will cascade, leading to timeout errors. Asynchronous queues solve this.

blocks.title: Message Queue Paradigm
blocks.content: In an async queue, Agent A writes a message to the broker and returns a taskId. Workers pick up the task and update the taskId state, decoupling the pipeline.

Question 1: What problem does asynchronous queueing solve in agent communications?
- A) It increases model temperatures.
- B) It prevents cascading timeouts by decoupling agent call requests from task completion.
- C) It compiles python bindings.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Decoupling stops network timeouts.
